conditions.displayName: OOM log signatures on Cloud Run service api

conditionMatchedLog.filter: resource.type="cloud_run_revision" resource.labels.service_name="api" severity>=WARNING (textPayload=~"Memory limit of .* exceeded" OR textPayload=~"Reached heap limit" OR textPayload=~"JavaScript heap out of memory" OR textPayload=~"SIGABRT" OR textPayload=~"signal: killed" OR jsonPayload.event="oom")

documentation.subject: [CRITICAL] api OOM / memory-limit exceeded
documentation.content:
## Severity: CRITICAL

The `api` Cloud Run service (Cloud Functions v2) emitted an out-of-memory signature. Common triggers:

- `Memory limit of <N> exceeded` — Cloud Run killed the container (raise memory or reduce per-request footprint).
- `Reached heap limit` / `JavaScript heap out of memory` — Node V8 heap exhaustion.
- `SIGABRT` / `signal: killed` — abnormal container termination, frequently memory-driven.

### Why it matters
OOM kills drop in-flight requests and cold-start the next revision, so this is paired with elevated error rate and latency.

### Runbook
1. Check memory utilization: `run.googleapis.com/container/memory/utilizations` for service `api` (see the optional companion metric policy in `monitoring/README.md`).
2. Inspect recent deploys / payload sizes (large ingest, PDF, or vector batches).
3. Mitigate: raise the function memory limit, then investigate the leak.
4. Runbook: https://runbooks.internal/${PROJECT_ID}/api-oom (replace with your runbook URL).

### Tuning
This is **log-based** and fires immediately — no dependency on Agent I telemetry. To suppress noise, narrow the regexes or raise `notificationRateLimit.period`.
